ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ɔmmɔ ɔsu Katɔti údie ukyuli na ɔ́lɛ-nwu na nwuna udu kenye. Údie-ma, na badi ɔnana ku uleku.
translated: The child of Katɔti went to the farm with his mother and saw a big snake. The mother, however, told him not to be afraid.

ref: He was sorry that he had made them,
text: Na úduo Unwoti ka údie ukyuli ɔ́kya kalɔ lɛlɔ. Na nwuna kayi kénu ɔlɛ geen.
translated: The Unwoti people have a tradition of playing the ɔ́kya during celebrations. And the elders teach the youth the ways of their ancestors.

ref: I will give you a son, who will be called Ishmael, because I have heard your cry for help. And someday I will give you so many descendants that no one will be able to count them all.

translated: And the man asked her, “Where is your husband?” And she said, “I don't have a husband.” Then the man asked her again, “Is that your son?” She said, “Yes, he is my son, and his father's name is Ismael. He is a hunter and lives in the bush.
